task_completion.description: Agent successfully logs into the OpenEMR system.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR dashboard, calendar view, or main navigation menu is visible after the login screen.

task_completion.description: Agent navigates to the specific dashboard for patient Jayson Fadel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Jayson Fadel' is clearly visible in the patient information header, title bar, or active patient frame.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the interface to create a new encounter.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Encounter' form, modal, or screen is visible, typically showing fields for Visit Category and Reason.

task_completion.description: Agent enters the specific reason for the visit into the form.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'Lower back pain' (or text containing both 'back' and 'pain') is visible in the 'Reason', 'Chief Complaint', or description input field.

task_completion.description: The encounter is successfully saved and appears in the patient's record.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Encounters' list or history table for Jayson Fadel displays a new entry with the current date and the reason 'Lower back pain', or a success message confirming the save is visible.

integrity.description: Agent interacts with the OpenEMR web interface directly.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser displaying the OpenEMR application, not a terminal or code editor performing the actions.

integrity.description: Agent does not use SQL or backend commands to insert data.
integrity.visual_evidence: No command line interfaces or database management tools are visible performing data insertion.

integrity.description: Agent uses the provided form fields to input data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Reason' text is visible being typed or present inside the standard OpenEMR form input fields.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient Name: Jayson Fadel. DOB: 1992-06-30. The encounter reason must include keywords 'back' and 'pain' (e.g., 'Lower back pain').
